provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content: This Act may be cited as the English Law (Extent of Application) Act.

provisions.title: Extent to which the Law of England is in force in the Republic
provisions.content: Subject to the provisions of the Constitution of Zambia and to any other written law— (a) the common law; and (b) the doctrines of equity; and (c) the statutes which were in force in England on the 17th August, 1911 (being the commencement of the Northern Rhodesia Order in Council, 1911); and (d) any statutes of later date than that mentioned in paragraph (c) in force in England, now applied to the Republic, or which hereafter shall be applied thereto by any Act or otherwise; shall be in force in the Republic. [As amended by Acts No. 24 of 1973 and 1 of 1991] [Cap. 1] History of this document 15 April 2011 amendment not yet applied Amended by English Law (Extent of Application) (Amendment) Act, 2011 31 December 2002 amendment not yet applied Amended by English Law (Extent of Application) (Amendment) Act, 2002 31 December 1996 this version Consolidation 08 March 1963 Commenced Related documents Is amended by English Law (Extent of Application) (Amendment) Act, 2002 English Law (Extent of Application) (Amendment) Act, 2011 [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Subscribe Subscribe to our newsletter for updates and news. Contact us Visit our Facebook Page Visit our Twitter/X profile About ZambiaLII ZambiaLII is a pioneer in the free access to law movement and serves as a reliable and timely source of judicial decisions, legislation and statutory instruments. Since 2012, ZambiaLII has been hosted by the Southern African Institute for Policy and Research (SAIPAR) , an independent, educational and development-oriented research centre. AfricanLII provides technical support to ZambiaLII. View the ZambiaLII Brochure Pocket Law Use ZambiaLII offline with Pocket Law .
